steps.title: Pinpoint the timeline to contradict
steps.content: The consensus claims the size-reducing mutation developed only within the last 20,000 years. To directly support the assertion that this explanation is flawed, a finding must place that mutation well before 20,000 years ago.

steps.title: Match timeline evidence to the assertion
steps.content: Choice C directly supports the assertion by documenting the body-size-influencing IGF1 mutation in 53,000-year-old Siberian wolf fossils — an early ancestor of dogs — demonstrating that this mutation predates the 20,000-year consensus threshold and therefore could not be a recent product of selective breeding. The other choices either isolate the gene (not the specific size-influencing mutation) in unrelated species (A), identify a different mutation affecting unrelated traits (B), or confirm the consensus timeline by placing the variant within the last 20,000 years (D). That matches choice C — C is correct.
